content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 24, 1987


Steven L. Dorsey
City Attorney for Carson
c/o Richards, Watson & Gershan
Thirty-Eighth Floor
333 South Hope Street
Los Angeles, CA 90071-1469

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File Nos. I-87-130 and I-87-131

Dear Mr. Dorsey:

You have requested informal assistance to provide you with
written confirmation of your analysis of reporting responsi-
bilities for two of your clients, Sylvia Muise and Harold
Homan. You have specifically advised me by telephone that you
are seeking informal assistance as you have not sought your
clients' specific authorization to seek formal written advice.1/

Your request for confirmation is a follow-up on our advice
letter to you on behalf of Sylvia Muise, No. A-86-248. That
letter is incorporated herein by reference as are your two
recent letters to me.

This letter will confirm our telephone conversation as
related in your two letters. You have accurately stated my
advice to you regarding the proper analysis of your clients'
respective reporting obligations under these circumstances.

I trust this satisfactorily responds to your request.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division

REL:km

1/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with
the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 8CO ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

RICHARDS, WATSON & GERSHON
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

THIRTY-EIGHTH FLOOR
333 SOUTH GRAND AVENUE
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90071/1469
(213) 626-8464

CABLE ADDRESS
RICHVAT

TELECOMER (213) 626-0078

April 28, 1987

Robert Leidigh, Esq.
Staff Attorney
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Re:  Statement of Economic Interests
     Filed by Howard Homan

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

I am writing this letter to confirm our telephone conversation at the end of March concerning the Statement of Economic Interests filed by Howard Homan, the Director of Parks and Recreation of the City of Carson.  Please contact me if this letter does not accurately reflect your understanding of our conversation, as I advised Mr. Homan to complete his Statement in accordance with that conversation.

As we discussed, Mr. Homan must list as a donor of a gift all persons who contributed $50 or more to the reception following the memorial service for Leonard Muise if the intent of the donor was to benefit Mr. Homan.  If the intent was to benefit Councilwoman Muise or honor her late husband, the donors would be listed instead on Mrs. Muise's Statement.

As I indicated to you, it appears from my understanding of the facts and from discussions with various persons who contributed to the reception that the intent of the contributors was to honor Leonard Muise.  As a result, I informed you that Mr. Homan would not be listing any of the contributors on his Statement covering the year 1986.

Jay's Catering has indicated to me that they intend to hold Mr. Homan responsible for the outstanding balance for the

Robert Leidigh, Esq.
April 28, 1987
Page 2


reception.  Under the circumstances, any person contributing to
the cost of the reception during 1987 will be presumed, unless
there is evidence to the contrary, to be intending to benefit Mr.
Homan.  As a result, any such contributors will be listed on Mr.
Homan's Statement of Economic Interests covering 1987.

     Thank you for assisting me in determining how to handle
disclosure of the reception.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Steven L. Dorsey

ATTORNEYS AT LAW

[stamped: APR 30 9 52 AM 07]

THIRTY-EIGHT FLOOR
333 SOUTH HOPE STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90071
(213) 626-6464

CABLE ADDRESS
RICHWAT

TELECOPIER (213) 626-0078

April 28, 1987


Robert Leidigh, Esq.
Staff Attorney
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Re:  Statement of Economic Interests
     Filed by Sylvia Muise

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

     I am sending you this letter to confirm various telephone
conversations we had at the end of March concerning how
Councilwoman Muise should disclose contributions to the reception
held following the memorial service for Leonard Muise.  Please
contact me if your recollection is different than mine, as I
advised Councilwoman Muise to complete her Statement in
accordance with my understanding of our discussions.

     The determinations we reached are as follows:

     1.   Councilwoman Muise had made a reasonable attempt under
the circumstances to discover all persons contributing $50 or
more toward the reception.  Mrs. Muise will amend her Statement
if any other contributors should subsequently be discovered.  In
this regard, I informed you that the numbers I have received from
the various sources I contacted are all consistent and appear to
be accurate.

     2.   Councilwoman Muise should disclose all persons who
contributed, prior to January 1, 1987, $50 or more toward the
reception if the intent of the donor was to benefit her or her
family or to honor Leonard Muise.  As we discussed, it appears to
me that the intent of all the contributors was to honor Leonard
Muise.  As a result, Councilwoman Muise would disclose all

Robert Leidigh, Esq.
April 28, 1987
Page 2


persons and companies contributing $50 or more toward the cost
of the reception.

     3.     Councilwoman Muise would disclose Howard Homan as an
intermediary for the entire cost of the reception.  Jay's
Catering will be listed as contributing an amount equal to the
discount they offered toward the reception.

     4.     Shirley Brown would be listed as an intermediary for
the total amount of the sums paid by the Leonard Muise Memorial
Fund to Jay's Catering.  Ms. Brown would also be listed
separately as a donor of the individual contribution she made to
the Leonard Muise Memorial Fund that exceeded $50.

     5.     Councilwoman Muise should list the anonymous $100
contribution provided to the Leonard Muise Memorial Fund and
indicate on her Statement that she will amend her Statement if
she ever learns the source of that contribution.

     Thank you for your assistance in helping me determine how to
advise Councilwoman Muise to complete her Statement of Economic
Interests.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Steven L. Dorsey
                                        Assistant City Attorney

SLD:kw
A027


cc:  Howard Homan

*                    *
        California
        Fair Political
        Practices Commission


                                                May 1,, 1987




Steven Dorsey
Richards, Watson & Gershon
333 South Hope Street, 38th Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90071-1469

                                                Re:  87-131

Dear Mr. Dorsey:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on April 30, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Robert Leidigm, an attorney in
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If your request is for informal assistance we will
answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation
18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) You also should be aware
that your letter and our response are public records which may
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

                                                Very truly yours,


                                                Diane M. Griffiths
                                                General Counsel

sections.conclusion: You have accurately stated my advice to you regarding the proper analysis of your clients' respective reporting obligations under these circumstances.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether city officials Howard Homan and Sylvia Muise must disclose contributions to a memorial service reception as gifts under the Political Reform Act's Statement of Economic Interests reporting requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that the requestor's analysis of the reporting responsibilities for both clients was accurate, specifically regarding how to treat contributions to the memorial service reception based on donor intent.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a compilation of multiple related letters. Primary FPPC response is dated June 24, 1987 (87-131) confirming informal telephone advice. No verbatim QUESTION section present; conclusion extracted from body of confirmation letter. Includes accompanying April 28 letters from city attorney to FPPC outlining the specific factual analysis and May 1 receipt acknowledgment letters. Informal assistance designation means requestor received no immunity per Section 83114.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether city officials Howard Homan and Sylvia Muise must disclose contributions to a memorial service reception as gifts under the Political Reform Act's Statement of Economic Interests reporting requirements.

CONCLUSION: You have accurately stated my advice to you regarding the proper analysis of your clients' respective reporting obligations under these circumstances.

embedding.summary: This is a collection of correspondence from June 1987 regarding FPPC advice on Statement of Economic Interests disclosure obligations for city officials related to contributions toward a memorial service reception. The FPPC's June 24, 1987 informal advice letter confirms the city attorney's telephone-based analysis of reporting requirements for clients Sylvia Muise and Howard Homan.
